# UC-22.13.31 · Malware Prevention Agent Coverage on BES Cyber Assets

## Description

We list in-scope hosts that have not reported antivirus or EDR health within the required interval.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes endpoint and sourcetypes WinDefender:Status, WinDefender:Status`, crowdstrike:hosts. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize host naming between inventory and endpoint tools. (2) Set stale threshold per vendor guidance. (3) Weekly report to operations. (4) Track remediation tickets. Indexes required: `index=endpoint`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinDefender:Status`, `sourcetype=WinDefender:Status``, `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`, `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts``; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=WinDefender:Status` or `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize host naming between inventory and endpoint tools. (2) Set stale threshold per vendor guidance. (3) Weekly report to operations. (4) Track remediation tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv
| rename hostname as host
| join type=left host [
    search index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Status OR sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts) earliest=-48h
    | stats latest(_time) as last_report by host
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_report) OR (now()-last_report)>172800,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table host, bes_system, last_report
```

Understanding this SPL

**Malware Prevention Agent Coverage on BES Cyber Assets (CIP-007-6 R3 Part 3.1)** — We list in-scope hosts that have not reported antivirus or EDR health within the required interval.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=WinDefender:Status` or `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **stale** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where stale=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bes_system, host, last_report, stale.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the official BES or cyber-asset list, the ESP edge firewall, and the same Windows or network data this search is built to mirror for that time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (uncovered hosts), Single value (percent reporting), Map (optional site)

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv
| rename hostname as host
| join type=left host [
    search index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Status OR sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts) earliest=-48h
    | stats latest(_time) as last_report by host
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_report) OR (now()-last_report)>172800,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table host, bes_system, last_report
```

## Visualization

Table (uncovered hosts), Single value (percent reporting), Map (optional site)
